posts.author: TheEconomist

posts.text:
The 2020s are now one of the most inflationary decades in history:

US CPI has averaged 4.0% so far in the 2020s, the highest decade average since the 1980s.

This is more than DOUBLE the average of the 2010s, at 1.8%, and well above the 1990s and 2000s.

Since 1950, the only only periods with higher inflation were the 1970s and 1980s, at 7.4% and 5.1% on average, respectively.

This comes as inflation has been above 2.0% for 64 of the 76 months so far this decade, peaking at 9.1% in June 2022.

By comparison, during the 2010s, inflation was above 2.0% for 49 of the 120 months, with a peak of 3.9% in September 2011.

Inflation remains way too high.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter
